comments: # Frontend files (not needed for backend-only Railway deployment)
structure.rawContent:
# Frontend files (not needed for backend-only Railway deployment)
frontend/
node_modules/
package.json
package-lock.json
*.log
.env
.env.local
__pycache__/
*.pyc
.venv/
.pytest_cache/
.git/
.gitignore

docker-compose.yml.description: Multi-service Docker Compose definition (neo4j, ollama, miroshark)

structure.rawContent:
services:
  neo4j:
    image: neo4j:5.15-community
    container_name: miroshark-neo4j
    environment:
      - NEO4J_AUTH=neo4j/${NEO4J_PASSWORD:?NEO4J_PASSWORD must be set in .env}
    ports:
      - "7474:7474"
      - "7687:7687"
    volumes:
      - neo4j_data:/data
    restart: unless-stopped

  ollama:
    image: ollama/ollama:latest
    container_name: miroshark-ollama
    ports:
      - "11434:11434"
    volumes:
      - ollama_data:/root/.ollama
    restart: unless-stopped

  miroshark:
    image: ghcr.io/aaronjmars/miroshark:latest
    # Mirror image (replace the address above if pulling is slow)
    # image: ghcr.nju.edu.cn/aaronjmars/miroshark:latest
    container_name: miroshark
    environment:
      - LLM_API_KEY=ollama
      - LLM_BASE_URL=http://ollama:11434/v1
      - LLM_MODEL_NAME=qwen2.5:32b
      - NEO4J_URI=bolt://neo4j:7687
      - NEO4J_USER=neo4j
      - NEO4J_PASSWORD=${NEO4J_PASSWORD:?NEO4J_PASSWORD must be set in .env}
      - EMBEDDING_MODEL=nomic-embed-text
      - EMBEDDING_BASE_URL=http://ollama:11434
      - OPENAI_API_KEY=ollama
      - OPENAI_API_BASE_URL=http://ollama:11434/v1
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
      - "5001:5001"
    depends_on:
      - neo4j
      - ollama
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./backend/uploads:/app/backend/uploads

volumes:
  neo4j_data:
  ollama_data:

Dockerfile.description: Main application Docker image build instructions

structure.rawContent:
FROM python:3.11

# Install Node.js (>= 18) and necessary tools
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends nodejs npm \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy uv from the official uv image
COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.9.26 /uv /uvx /bin/

WORKDIR /app

# Copy dependency descriptor files first to leverage Docker cache
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
COPY frontend/package.json frontend/package-lock.json ./frontend/
COPY backend/pyproject.toml backend/uv.lock ./backend/

# Install dependencies (Node + Python)
RUN npm ci \
  && npm ci --prefix frontend \
  && cd backend && uv sync --frozen

# Copy project source code
COPY . .

EXPOSE 3000 5001

# Start both frontend and backend simultaneously (development mode)
CMD ["npm", "run", "dev"]

.dockerignore.purpose: Optimize Docker build context for backend-only Railway deployment

.dockerignore.relationship: dockerignore controls which files Dockerfile's COPY . . includes; excludes frontend/ since Railway deployment is backend-only

docker-compose.yml.relationship: docker-compose.yml's miroshark service uses Dockerfile (implied via build context); the miroshark image is built from the Dockerfile or pulled from ghcr.io

Dockerfile.relationship: Dockerfile is the build target for the miroshark service in docker-compose.yml; .dockerignore filters its build context

.dockerignore.purpose: Optimize Docker build context by excluding unnecessary files
keyFindings: Excludes frontend/ directory (frontend built separately for Railway deployment)
Excludes node_modules/, package.json, package-lock.json (dependencies installed inside container)
Excludes Python artifacts: __pycache__/, *.pyc, .venv/
Excludes .env, .env.local (sensitive credentials not baked into image)
Excludes .git/ and .gitignore (version control metadata not needed at runtime)
Excludes build/test artifacts: .pytest_cache/, *.log
securityConcerns: Ensures .env files are not included in build context, preventing secret leakage
recommendations: Consider adding backend/uploads/ exclusion if using local volume mounts for uploads
Review if any additional development artifacts should be excluded

docker-compose.yml.description: Multi-service Docker Compose definition (neo4j, ollama, miroshark)
docker-compose.yml.purpose: Define and orchestrate the MiroShark multi-service architecture
keyFindings: Three services defined: neo4j (graph DB), ollama (LLM runtime), miroshark (main app)
Neo4j 5.15-community used as the knowledge graph database
Ollama runs locally with qwen2.5:32b model for LLM inference
NEO4J_PASSWORD must be set via .env file (required variable)
Miroshark service connects to neo4j at bolt://neo4j:7687 and ollama at http://ollama:11434
Uses nomic-embed-text as the embedding model via Ollama
Named volumes: neo4j_data and ollama_data for data persistence
Bind mount: ./backend/uploads mapped to /app/backend/uploads
securityConcerns: NEO4J_PASSWORD required via environment - ensure .env is secure
Ollama exposed on host network port 11434 without authentication
Neo4j exposed on host network ports without built-in auth configuration beyond password
recommendations: Add internal network to isolate services from host where possible
Consider adding health checks for all services
Document how to pre-pull qwen2.5:32b and nomic-embed-text models for Ollama
Consider resource limits for services to prevent host resource exhaustion

Dockerfile.description: Main application Docker image build instructions
Dockerfile.purpose: Build the MiroShark application container image for deployment
keyFindings: Based on python:3.11 official image
Installs Node.js and npm via apt-get for frontend dependency management
Multi-stage copy from ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.9.26 to include uv package manager
Dependency-first copy strategy: package files copied first for Docker layer caching
Installs both Node (npm ci) and Python (uv sync --frozen) dependencies
Exposes ports 3000 (frontend) and 5001 (backend)
Runs in development mode via CMD: npm run dev
Uses WORKDIR /app for the application directory
securityConcerns: Runs as root by default (python:3.11 base image behavior)
Development mode enabled - CMD runs npm run dev which uses hot reloading
Full apt-get update/install without pinning package versions
recommendations: Add a non-root user for production deployment
Separate dev and production Dockerfiles (or use multi-stage build)
Pin apt package versions for reproducible builds
Add .dockerignore reference to ensure build context is minimal
Consider Alpine-based Python image for smaller image size
